trends.name: Divergence as Investment Paradigm
trends.description: Global economy entering a period of structural divergence across countries, asset classes, and sectors — replacing the synchronized post-pandemic recovery with differentiated performance trajectories.

signals.name: US Mid-Caps as Overlooked Opportunity
signals.description: Fidelity analysts identifying US mid-cap equities as undervalued relative to large-cap tech concentration — a thematic signal of attention shifting beyond the AI-dominated mega-cap narrative.

signals.name: Pro-Innovation US Administration Signaling
signals.description: Post-2024 US election administration posture toward innovation and business signaling a more permissive environment for technology investment and deployment.

trends.name: Authenticity as Commercial Film Premium
trends.description: Commercial filmmaking audiences becoming more discerning — demanding content that feels emotionally authentic and resonant over polished production, raising the bar for brand storytelling.

signals.name: Rapid Trend Cycle in Commercial Production
signals.description: Commercial filmmaking trend cycles accelerating, requiring brands and agencies to adapt creative approaches continuously rather than relying on established visual languages for extended periods.

trends.name: AI Regulatory Compliance Complexity Escalating
trends.description: Legal and regulatory complexity around AI deployment — spanning privacy, data portability, cybersecurity, and liability — accelerating faster than most organizations' compliance infrastructure.

trends.name: Data Litigation as Emerging Risk Category
trends.description: Data-related litigation emerging as a significant enterprise risk category, with class actions, regulatory enforcement, and private claims creating new legal exposure for data-intensive businesses.

signals.name: Privacy Law Convergence Emerging Globally
signals.description: Global privacy regulatory frameworks beginning to show common elements despite different jurisdictions — creating both compliance opportunities and the risk of complex multi-regime requirements.

signals.name: Data Portability Rights Expanding
signals.description: Consumer data portability rights expanding across jurisdictions, creating new obligations for data holders and opportunities for platforms that make data transfer seamless.

trends.name: AI Form Factor Problem Unsolved
trends.description: Generative AI's full potential constrained by the absence of a unified form factor that truly embodies AI-enabled experiences — siloed apps remain the dominant delivery mechanism.

trends.name: Optimism as Innovation Prerequisite
trends.description: Design and innovation leaders framing positive futures vision as a prerequisite to meaningful transformation — optimism as methodology and competitive tool, not sentiment.

signals.name: AI Impact on Human Vision — Literal and Metaphorical
signals.description: frog's Futurescape identifying AI's effect on how humans perceive, frame, and generate visual experiences as one of 25 key forces transforming the next quarter-century.

trends.name: Technology Supercycle
trends.description: Convergence of AI, biotechnology, and a third transformative force creating an extended supercycle of technological change — more potent and pervasive than prior waves, reshaping every industry simultaneously.

trends.name: Biotech-AI Convergence
trends.description: Artificial intelligence and biotechnology converging to accelerate drug discovery, personalized medicine, and biological engineering — creating a combined field that transforms healthcare and life sciences.

signals.name: Every Technology Category Under Simultaneous Transformation
signals.description: FTI's 2024 analysis showing AR/VR, AI, biotech, energy, mobility, computing, and more all experiencing significant inflections simultaneously — reinforcing the supercycle thesis.

signals.name: Strategic Foresight as Competitive Differentiator
signals.description: FTI arguing that trend data alone offers limited foresight — the interplay between trends and strategic anticipation of futures is where genuine competitive advantage is generated.

entries.filename: G7 - Driving Factors and Challenges of AI Adoption and Development.pdf

trends.name: AI Adoption Gap for Small Businesses
trends.description: G7 analysis identifying a structural gap in AI adoption between large enterprises and micro/small businesses — threatening to widen economic inequality as AI productivity gains concentrate at scale.

signals.name: AI Applications in Manufacturing SMEs
signals.description: Small and medium manufacturers beginning to adopt AI for production process optimization, signaling that AI's industrial use is extending beyond large enterprise into the broader economy.

signals.name: Computational Capacity as AI Barrier for SMEs
signals.description: Lack of computational infrastructure identified as a primary barrier to AI adoption among micro and small enterprises, creating demand for cloud-based AI access models.

trends.name: Overbranding — Brandification of Everyday Life
trends.description: Brands extending their presence into all fields of human existence enabled by digital technology — with consumers in an increasingly distrustful world questioning the appropriate limits of brand intrusion.

signals.name: AI-Made Content Labeling Emerging
signals.description: Brands beginning to tag content as 'AI-Made' to differentiate non-human content — an early signal of disclosure norms forming around AI-generated brand communication.

trends.name: Design as Agent of Positive Change
trends.description: Architecture and design industry positioning design as a primary tool for addressing climate, social, and economic challenges — with 2025 marked by renewed client investment after a cautious period.

trends.name: AI and AR Transforming Design Process
trends.description: AI and augmented reality tools fundamentally changing how designers work, prototype, and deliver — accelerating iterations and enabling new forms of client collaboration and spatial visualization.

signals.name: Interest Rate Easing Unlocking Design Investment
signals.description: Global interest rate declines in 2025 enabling clients to move off the sidelines and commit to new building and design projects that were deferred during the high-rate environment.
